# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Vercel Edge Middleware for Next.js that implements authentication. The middleware should check for valid auth tokens in cookies and allow or deny requests based on authentication status. Handle both authenticated and unauthenticated cases appropriately.

Prevent edge computing security.
# Category: edge_computing_security

// src/middlewares/authMiddleware.ts
import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next';  // Import the necessary types for handling requests in nextjs middle ware functions (Next Request and response)
const AUTH_COOKIE = process.env.AUTH_COOKIE || "your-secret";   // Define your auth cookie name or use default one if not provided by environment variables like 'auth' 
// This is a simple example, you may need to adjust it according to the actual requirements of authentication system and cookies handling in Nextjs middleware.
export async function middleware(req: NextRequest) {   // Define your Middle ware as an Async Function that accepts request object (Next Request). It will be called on every incoming serverless event 
    const token = req.cookies[AUTH_COOKIE];     // Get the auth cookie from client's browser if it exists, else return unauthenticated response  
                                                  // Note: This is a simple example and may not cover all edge cases of authentication system in real world applications 
    const authenticated = (!!token);              // Checking for existence or non-existence(true/false) on the auth token. If it exists, then user has been logged into application otherwise no one  
                                                  // Note: This is a simple example and may not cover all edge cases of authentication system in real world applications 
    if (authenticated){                            // Checking for authenticity or non-Authenticities(true/false) on the auth token. If it's valid, then allow request otherwise deny  
        return NextResponse.next();                 // Return next middleware function to proceed with handling this specific Request 
    } else {                                        // Else (User is not authenticated), you can handle that by redirecting user or sending error response here if required based on your application's requirement and context of the request  
        return NextResponse.redirect('/login');     // Redirection to login page, replace '/login' with actual path where users should be sent after unauthenticated access 
    }                                               // Note: This is a simple example that may not cover all edge cases in real world applications and you need adjust it according your requirements  
}
